title: A Big Night for Movies at the Oscars
summary: The Oscars are a big awards show for movies. This year, a movie called One Battle After Another won six awards, including best picture. Paul Thomas Anderson, the director, won his first Oscar. Michael B Jordan won best actor for his role in Sinners, where he played twins in the 1930s. Jessie Buckley won her first Oscar for playing Shakespeare's wife in Hamnet. She dedicated her award to mothers and her daughter. Sean Penn also won an award but was not there to get it. The Oscars celebrate great movies and actors.

What did Jessie Buckley dedicate her award to?
How many awards did One Battle After Another win?
Who was not present to accept his award?

background_read: The Oscars, or Academy Awards, are annual awards given by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences to honor achievements in the film industry. They include categories like best picture, best actor, and best director. Winning an Oscar is a big honor for filmmakers and actors, as it recognizes their hard work and talent. The ceremony is watched by millions of people around the world and celebrates movies from the past year.
Article_Structure: WHO: Paul Thomas Anderson, Michael B Jordan, Jessie Buckley, Sean Penn, and the movie One Battle After Another
WHAT: They won awards at the Oscars, including best picture, best actor, and best supporting actor
WHERE: At the Oscars ceremony, a big event for movies
WHY: To celebrate and honor great movies and actors for their work

perspectives.description: The article shows how the Oscars celebrate actors and filmmakers for their hard work and achievements.
